hypothesis:
After 19 iterations, three facts are now definitive: (1) cross-sectional ranking on any ETF universe (8 or 20) with weekly/biweekly rebalancing produces <15 trades — the top-N basket is simply too sticky; (2) daily rebalancing destroys edge via transaction costs; (3) absolute momentum filters kill trade count. The solution requires a completely different signal architecture.

This iteration abandons cross-sectional ranking entirely and instead uses a HYBRID RSI + Z-SCORE MEAN REVERSION signal applied independently to each of the 8 ETFs. The key insight: mean reversion signals fire frequently (multiple times per month per ETF) but only when backed by a short-term trend filter to avoid catching falling knives.

Signal architecture:
1. **Entry (buy) for each ETF independently when ALL hold**:
   - 5-day RSI < 35 (short-term oversold — captures liquidity-driven dislocations)
   - 10-day z-score of price < -1.0 (price is statistically below recent mean — adds confirmation)
   - 20-day SMA is above 60-day SMA (medium-term uptrend — trade WITH the trend)

2. **Exit when EITHER**:
   - 5-day RSI > 55 (mean reversion confirmed, momentum restored)
   - OR position held > 6 days (time-based stop to cap drawdown)

Why this generates 50+ trades:
- RSI < 35 fires ~8-12x per year per ETF in normal market conditions
- 8 ETFs × 10 entries/year × 5 IS years = 400 potential entries
- Even at 40% filter efficiency (20-day SMA > 60-day SMA gate) = 160 trades in IS
- This is 3x the 50-trade minimum

Why this improves Sharpe:
- RSI reversal knowledge base shows IS Sharpe ~1.2 on liquid ETFs with 5-day RSI
- Z-score confirmation reduces false signals (complementary signal)
- Trend filter (20d > 60d SMA) ensures we only buy dips in uptrending markets
- Short holding period (max 6 days) limits exposure to regime changes
- Long-only avoids short-side losses
- Low turnover per trade (2 trades per round trip, 6-day avg hold) vs daily rebalancing that destroyed edge in iters 4/7/17

evaluation.post_mortem: Failed transaction cost gate: post-cost IS Sharpe = -0.16. Pre-cost IS Sharpe was -0.03. The edge is too thin to survive realistic trading costs.
evaluation.next_direction: Strategy needs a larger edge. Reduce trade frequency or focus on higher-conviction signals.
